action.reasoning: Observation: iter_3 trained with variance-weighted loss, val_r2=0.5729 (internal weighted metric). The predict.py now zeros out near-zero variance outputs. Need to submit eval to verify the fix works. Analysis: The zero_mask approach should fix the catastrophic R² issue. But I also notice the variance-weighted loss is causing instability and lower performance than simple MSE (iter_1 got 0.9178 internal R²). I should switch back to simple MSE loss but keep the zero_mask for predictions. Also need to fix the val_r2 computation to use true unweighted mean R². Decision: Submit eval with current checkpoint first, then retrain with simple MSE + zero_mask.
